Anticipating McAfee wanting to test the texting function 6.23.26 KK - connected with Tania - no large risks from the call to note. Alexa is going to share an executive note to Nick 6.19.26 KK - alexa shared email regarding reference feedback from aitor. Secure updarte on POC agreement on 4.21 from Alejandro. 4.16.26 KC: Email Alejandro to determine status of securing signature on POC agreement by 4.17. 4.13.26 KC: Engage Ronit on Questionnaire progress
hard.ais_why:
• The products on this deal are iSaaS, Agentic AI, and ANA, and Zycus has flagged AI as Very Critical for the account. The 5/4/2026 next-step note records the McAfee ANA POC launched on 5/5. On the 5/5/2025 Avoma call, the buyer confirmed direct interest in Zycus's autonomous negotiation and spend dynamics capabilities.
• The 1/19/2026 rep note captures McAfee wanting to see whether the vendor can deliver on its Agentic AI innovation claims. The 10/21/2025 note documents Aitor pushing on integrating Zycus's autonomous negotiation with Omnia for indirect tail-spend orchestration, with detailed back-and-forth on AI accuracy, hallucination prevention, benchmark confidence, and human-in-the-loop design.
• The buyer's own POC scope requires an evidence-based business case validated through real-world use cases, plus scalability and operational viability post-POC across categories. McAfee tightened the shortlist from 9 vendors to 3 explicitly for the AI and ANA evaluation, which is enterprise-grade commitment, not curiosity.

north_star_verdict.headline: A genuinely engaged, weekly-cadence ANA POC with a top AI-fit signal, but the 31 July close cannot hold: no POC use case has completed end-to-end, the ANA commercial proposal and the economic buyer (CFO) are still open, and Aitor flagged supplier responsiveness as a potential structural limitation on 24 June. The deal is good; the date is optimistic (realistic close ~September 2026).
north_star_verdict.math: Created 6 May 2025, ~14 months in cycle, in Shortlisted. Close 31 July 2026 = 25 days out (swept 6 July 2026). Deal is LIVE: weekly ANA PoC follow-ups scheduled through 23 July, buyer emails through 2 July. But 0 of the POC use cases have completed end-to-end, the ANA-only commercial proposal is undelivered, the CFO is unmapped, and the executive go/no-go is undated. CloseDate already slipped twice (4 Jun -> 30 Jun on 5 Jun; 30 Jun -> 31 Jul on 29 Jun). A forward slip of ~60 days is required.
evidence: Aitor Gonzalez Martin (McAfee) email 1 July 2026: 'I agree this is the right approach, particularly in allowing suppliers to respond via email as well as through the portal. Do you have an estimate for when this might ' - buyer is positive on the supplier-response fix and now awaiting a delivery date from Zycus.

Aitor Gonzalez Martin email 24 June 2026: supplier responsiveness in the live POC is a potential structural limitation; new use cases are being relaunched with test suppliers to accelerate results - no use case has completed end-to-end yet.

Next Step 11 June 2026: 'McAfee has an internal meeting planned with CEO/CIO for week of june 24 to review the ANA video... finalize evaluation of this initial use case within the next 1-2 weeks' - a validation gate, not a budget approval.
Next Step 4 June 2026: 'executive session w leadership - TBD' - the decision gate still has no date.
Field history 29 June 2026: CloseDate pushed 30 June -> 31 July by Karson Keogh, the second slip in a month.
Weekly ANA PoC follow-up events scheduled 9, 16 and 23 July 2026 plus buyer emails through 2 July 2026 confirm the deal is engaged and live.

metrics.narrative: McAfee's objective is to stop skilled buyers manually negotiating low-value indirect and tactical spend: Aitor Gonzalez Martin (21 October 2025) said services are 'what we most spend... that's critical for us' and the buyer wants to push the autonomous threshold from roughly $50,000 toward $100,000. Miles (McAfee excellence center) framed the throughput pain on 21 October 2025: 'somebody on our team can do 10 negotiations today... by using Zycus they can now do 50'. Quantified impact so far is vendor-supplied only (Devika, 21 October 2025: 4-6% tail-spend savings, 70-80% negotiation-time reduction); McAfee has not captured its own baseline because no POC use case has completed end-to-end, so building the McAfee-specific savings/throughput number is the gating move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in Salesforce or engaged directly. A CFO meeting was referenced once (Next Step 11 June 2026: 'McAfee meeting w CFO on Monday') with no readout, and an internal CEO/CIO review of the ANA video was set for the week of 24 June 2026 - both are validation touchpoints, not confirmed budget sponsorship. Nick Priest (Head of Global Procurement, OpportunityContactRole) is the most senior mapped buyer and receives updates and reference feedback (last touched 2 July 2026), but budget authority above him is unmapped. Alexa Bradley's executive note to Nick (Next Step 19 June 2026) is the current bridge toward power. Because the deal is Shortlisted with commercials pending, the un-engaged budget owner is the real mid-stage risk; naming and dating a CFO sponsorship session is a ranked move.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is an evidence-based POC: McAfee's own scope requires a business case validated through real-world use cases plus scalability and operational viability post-POC across categories (AIS_Why__c; POC agreement April 2026). Aitor Gonzalez Martin repeatedly named the criteria that matter most on 21 October 2025 - coverage of indirect and tactical/intangible services rather than just tailspend, autonomous-negotiation accuracy with a human-in-the-loop, benchmark confidence ('I prefer for Anna to be using the benchmark rather than our own data'), and hallucination/supplier-legitimacy controls. Supplier responsiveness inside the live event has since become an explicit acceptance criterion (Aitor, 24 June - 1 July 2026), with email/portal response now agreed as the right approach.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call and activity sequence: McAfee ran discovery (17 October 2025), a capability demo (21 October 2025), signed a POC agreement (April 2026), and is now in a weekly-cadence live POC with reference checks (Nippon Gas 18 June 2026, Tata Play pending) and an internal ANA-video review (week of 24 June 2026). The buyer stated it wants to finalize the initial use case within 1-2 weeks, then test two additional use cases before a go/no-go (Next Step 11 June 2026). The executive go/no-go session remains undated ('executive session w leadership - TBD', 4 June 2026) and the approval chain above procurement is not documented; Aitor has said deployment would be 'ASAP' if the POC business case succeeds (21 October 2025).

paper_process.narrative: A mutual NDA was signed (Aitor referenced it on the 21 October 2025 call) and a POC agreement was routed for signature in April 2026 via Alejandro Lanzavecchia. Commercial structure was outlined only verbally - spend-tier ANA licensing with a separate integration scope of work, single integration point (Alexa Bradley, 21 October 2025: 'the integration would be separately... our implementation scope of work'). No written commercial proposal, MSA/SOW, or InfoSec review is yet in motion, though Luis (McAfee) flagged on 21 October 2025 he wants to 'double click on that with the relevant stakeholder is an ANA-only single-module deal, so expect an Order Form plus SOW rather than a full-suite MSA; the path from POC success to signature is not formalized and no paper owner is named, and there is no contract-expiration forcing function.
sources: Avoma demo 21 October 2025 (Aitor, Alexa Bradley, Luis)

identify_pain.narrative: McAfee underwent an ~18-month transformation (SAP to Workday, Omnia orchestration) and now wants to automate low-value tactical and tailspend sourcing so skilled buyers stop spending weeks on small negotiations (Aitor Gonzalez Martin, 17 October 2025: 'we wanna try... autonomous sourcing... internally we don't have that'). Aitor owns the pain as the evaluation lead and is explicit that services/indirect coverage, not just tailspend, is critical ('that's what we most spend... that's critical for us', 21 October 2025), while Miles quantified the throughput squeeze (10 negotiations today vs a backlog of 60). Pain is real, owner-attributed, and reaffirmed through the live POC.

champion.narrative: Aitor Gonzalez Martin is the active, agenda-driving champion - he initiated the outreach, scopes every session, drives the POC and gives candid two-way feedback (17 October 2025 through 1 July 2026, including agreeing on 1 July that the email/portal supplier-response approach is 'the right approach'). Mazen El-Haidari (runs McAfee's procurement excellence center, Houston) is a supportive second thread with executive visibility via the CPONext/Horizon feature (12 June 2026). The gap: neither has a proven bridge to the budget owner, so the relationship is concentrated on two mid-level transformation owners. Champion strength is developing - strong on the evaluation, unproven on power - and the POC must prove out for Aitor's advocacy to convert into a signature.

competition.narrative: The strongest current threat is the do-nothing / build-on-existing-stack path (Workday sourcing + Omnia orchestration, freshly deployed) combined with genuine AI-readiness skepticism - until ANA completes a use case end-to-end, the status quo wins by default (Aitor, 17-21 October 2025: 'are we ready for this kind of solution?'; reinforced by the 24 June 2026 supplier-responsiveness concern). McAfee tightened the shortlist from 9 vendors to 3, and one or two peer POC vendors remain but have never been named (Aitor, 17 October 2025: 'one or two vendors') - a real knowledge gap. Zycus's wedge is unified source-to-pay scalability, native Microsoft Teams intake, and early-adopter agentic-negotiation ROI, activated by winning the relaunched POC on McAfee's own data and closing the services/intangible-spend accuracy gap Aitor pressed on.

deal_movement.summary: Live, weekly-cadence POC. Since the last sweep the supplier-response (WhatsApp/SMS/email) enhancement was shipped to Aitor and Nick (29 June) and Aitor endorsed the approach on 1 July, now awaiting a delivery-date estimate; CloseDate slipped 30 June -> 31 July (29 June). Reference track advanced (Nippon Gas call done 18 June); Tata Play, the buyer's #1, still pending.

items.change: Aitor Gonzalez Martin endorsed the email/portal supplier-response approach and asked Zycus for a delivery-date estimate
Zycus responded in detail to Aitor's activity-feedback points
Shared supplier-response (WhatsApp, SMS, email) enhancement to Aitor and Nick Priest; McAfee anticipated to test the texting function
CloseDate pushed 30 June -> 31 July by Karson Keogh (second slip in a month)
Nippon Gas reference call completed; Aitor appreciated it but wants a reference closer to McAfee's own use case

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing / status-quo path (Workday sourcing + Omnia orchestration): with no POC use case completed end-to-end and supplier responsiveness flagged as a potential structural limitation on 24 June 2026, the status quo wins by default until ANA proves out. One or two peer POC vendors remain in the final 3 but are still unnamed (17 October 2025), a knowledge gap that blocks tailored positioning. The Workday/Omnia stack is also the incumbent Zycus must integrate with, not replace. Zip is dormant since May 2025.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / existing Workday + Omnia stack
competitors.quote: are we ready for this kind of solution? Is our procurement function ready?
competitors.how_we_win: Win the relaunched POC on McAfee's own data: close the supplier-responsiveness gap (WhatsApp/SMS/email reminders shipped 29 June), complete at least one use case end-to-end, and quantify savings and throughput so the ROI beats the status quo.

competitors.name: Unnamed peer POC vendor(s) in the final 3
competitors.quote: one or two vendors, and see the results
competitors.how_we_win: Get Aitor or Nick to name the peer, then differentiate on full source-to-pay scalability and native Teams intake versus a point tool; surface it live in the POC head-to-head.

competitors.name: Incumbent stack (Workday sourcing + Omnia orchestration)
competitors.quote: we're using Omnia as an intake for purchases... that logic will remain in Omnia
competitors.how_we_win: Position ANA as an overlay that integrates to Omnia/Workday (single integration point, lower implementation cost), not a rip-and-replace.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: we're looking at things in the market like Zip, like Zycus
competitors.how_we_win: Not referenced since May 2025; monitor only - emphasize unified platform versus orchestration-only point tools.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong AI/agentic fit on tailspend, but McAfee's decisive criterion is services/intangible spend coverage, where Zycus openly conceded lower accuracy - the exposure that must be closed in the POC.

best_practice_check.summary: Mid-stage POC that is engaged but not yet proven; the win levers are proving ANA end-to-end, mapping the budget owner, and getting the buyer's own numbers into the business case.
flags: Complete one POC use case end-to-end and capture McAfee's own savings/throughput numbers - without a McAfee-specific business case the status-quo/do-nothing path wins by default (Aitor flagged supplier responsiveness as a structural limitation, 24 June).
Map and engage the budget owner: a CFO meeting and CEO/CIO review were referenced but never converted to a dated sponsorship session, so power sits above the two mid-level champions (Aitor, Mazen).
Close the services/intangible-spend exposure with a live services demo - it is the buyer's most-spent category and the one criterion where Zycus conceded lower accuracy (Aitor, 21 October 2025).

champion_strength.summary: Aitor Gonzalez Martin is an active, agenda-driving champion with Mazen El-Haidari a supportive second thread. Developed: strong advocacy and two-way engagement through 1 July 2026. Missing: a proven bridge to the economic buyer and budget sponsorship.
champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 17-21 October 2025; CPONext 12 June 2026; buyer emails through 2 July 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS 9.0 / Strong. Agentic-negotiation narrative (Merlin/ANA, native Teams intake) is landing on tailspend; the positioning gap is proving services/intangible-spend accuracy, which Zycus openly conceded is not yet there.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 9.0, AIS_Status__c Strong, AI flagged Very Critical. Calls corroborate strong appetite: McAfee narrowed 9 vendors to 3 specifically for the AI/ANA evaluation and Aitor pressed hard on accuracy, hallucination prevention, and human-in-the-loop. The field is accurately rated, not over-stated.

items.detail: 31 July close is not credible: no POC use case completed end-to-end, executive go/no-go undated, CloseDate slipped twice in a month
Economic buyer (CFO) unmapped and not directly engaged; power sits above the two mid-level champions
ANA must integrate with, not replace, Omnia orchestration and Workday requisition; single integration point but accuracy on complex/intangible services still unproven

Nippon Gas reference done but not close enough to McAfee's use case; Tata Play (buyer's #1) still pending

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and engaged - weekly POC follow-ups through 23 July and buyer emails through 2 July - but momentum is timing-constrained, not cooling.
items: Buyer endorsed the supplier-response fix and asked for a delivery date (Aitor, 1 July 2026) - active pull.
Weekly ANA PoC follow-up events booked 9, 16, 23 July 2026.
CloseDate slipped twice in a month - a pace/scope signal, not disengagement.

customer_preference.evidence: Deep, sustained Zycus-specific POC engagement and Aitor's 1 July endorsement ('I agree this is the right approach'), but no explicit preference over the unnamed peer vendors and an open services-fit concern keep it short of high.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and vendor benchmarks only (Devika: 4-6% tail-spend savings, 70-80% negotiation-time reduction, 21 October 2025); McAfee's own baseline is uncaptured because no POC use case has completed end-to-end.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A top-AI-fit ANA POC that McAfee is genuinely running weekly, but it earns a mid-50s win read, not more, because zero use cases have completed end-to-end, the CFO/budget owner is unmapped, and the do-nothing status quo wins by default until ANA proves out on McAfee's own data.

win_position.text: Win reads ~56 (Shortlisted caps confidence near 70): AIS 9/Strong and an active champion in Aitor Gonzalez Martin pull it up, but no completed POC use case and an unmapped economic buyer hold it well below the cap.

The economic buyer is a gap - a CFO meeting and CEO/CIO review were referenced (Next Step 11 June) but never converted to a dated sponsorship session, so budget authority above Nick Priest is unengaged.

The biggest threat is status quo: Aitor flagged supplier responsiveness as a potential structural limitation on 24 June and no use case has finished end-to-end, so the do-nothing / Workday+Omnia path wins by default until ANA delivers measurable results.

deal_momentum.text: Live weekly cadence: ANA PoC follow-ups are booked 9, 16 and 23 July and Aitor endorsed the supplier-response fix and asked for a delivery date on 1 July - a buyer pull, not a push.
CloseDate slipped twice in a month (4 Jun to 30 Jun, then 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 29 June) and the executive go/no-go is still 'TBD', so momentum is real but the date is not.

customer_commitment.text: McAfee narrowed 9 vendors to 3 for the AI/ANA evaluation, signed the POC agreement (April 2026), and is relaunching use cases with test suppliers - enterprise-grade commitment to the evaluation.

Commitment stops short of budget: deployment is explicitly conditional on POC success ('it depends on how well it goes to POC', Aitor 21 October 2025) and the written commercial proposal is still undelivered.

deal_risk.text: Services/intangible-spend accuracy - McAfee's most-spent category - is the one criterion where Zycus conceded it is 'not that accurate' (Devika, 21 October 2025), so the POC must close that exposure to win.
Reference risk: the Nippon Gas call landed but was not close enough to McAfee's use case, and Tata Play (the buyer's #1) is still pending (Aitor, 24 June 2026).

economic_buyer_engaged.evidence: CFO/budget owner referenced but never engaged; executive session TBD (Next Step 4-11 June 2026).

proof_of_value.evidence: No POC use case completed end-to-end; McAfee-specific savings uncaptured (Aitor email 24 June 2026).

timeline_credibility.evidence: CloseDate slipped twice in a month; 31 July not credible on current pace.

scope_change.from: Full source-to-pay suite exploration (Kevin/Devika framed the end-to-end suite)

scope_change.detail: The buyer explicitly narrowed to Anna standalone: 'since we're not purchasing the end-to-end lot of modules, we're just focusing on one' (Aitor) and 'just iAnalyze' (Alexa Bradley), 21 October 2025. A defensive, phased single-module entry rather than a full-suite buy - keeps deal value and change-management risk contained but caps initial ACV.

reasons.summary: Scope shrinking vs prior — The buyer explicitly narrowed to Anna standalone: 'since we're not purchasing the end-to-end lot of modules, we're just focusing on one' (Aitor) and 'just iAnalyze' (Alexa Bradley), 21 October 2025. A. Buyer likely getting defensive on cost or implementation/integration (phased over big-bang) — watch.
reasons.detail: The buyer explicitly narrowed to Anna standalone: 'since we're not purchasing the end-to-end lot of modules, we're just focusing on one' (Aitor) and 'just iAnalyze' (Alexa Bradley), 21 October 2025. A defensive, phased single-module entry rather than a full-suite buy - keeps deal value and change-management risk contained but caps initial ACV.

reasons.to: ANA (autonomous negotiation) single module on tail/tactical spend, single integration point

reasons.detail: This $200K Best Case (forecasted) opportunity had its close date pushed from Jun 30 to Jul 31 on 29 June, and the ANA POC still shows 0 of 5 use cases completed with supplier-responsiveness flagged as a structural gap. No economic buyer is mapped (only Nick Priest, Head of Global Procurement, is engaged; the CFO is unnamed), so there is no confirmed budget sponsor behind the slip. If the quarter's forecast leans on McAfee, a stalled POC plus an unproven CFO means the deal likely pushes again and the status-quo Workday/Omnia stack wins by default.
reasons.metric: Close date pushed 31 days (Jun 30 -> Jul 31); POC 0 of 5 use cases
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Karson why McAfee's close date slipped to Jul 31, whether any of the 5 POC use cases will land before month-end, and who the real economic buyer/CFO is behind a forecasted $200K deal.
reasons.summary: Forecasted McAfee deal pushed close date a month (Jun 30 -> Jul 31), POC 0 of 5 proven
reasons.evidence: CloseDate changed 2026-06-30 -> 2026-07-31 by Karson Keogh on 2026-06-29; competitive_position: '0 of 5 POC use cases completed... status quo wins by default until ANA proves out'.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — The buyer explicitly narrowed to Anna standalone: 'since we're not purchasing the end-to-end lot of modules, we're just focusing on one' (Aitor) and 'just iAnal (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)

Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 46 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); momentum 59 vs stage-expected 56. Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — The buyer explicitly narrowed to Anna standalone: 'since we're not purchasing the end-to-end lot of modules,….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 28 is anchored on the stage win (46), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: A top-AI-fit ANA POC that McAfee is genuinely running weekly, but it earns a mid-50s win read, not more, because zero use cases have completed end-to-end, the CFO/budget owner is unmapped, and the do-nothing status quo wins by default until ANA proves out on McAfee's own data.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Win reads ~56 (Shortlisted caps confidence near 70): AIS 9/Strong and an active champion in Aitor Gonzalez Martin pull it up, but no completed POC use case and an unmapped economic buyer hold it well below the cap.

The economic buyer is a gap - a CFO meeting and CEO/CIO review were referenced (Next Step 11 June) but never converted to a dated sponsorship session, so budget authority above Nick Priest is unengaged.

The biggest threat is status quo: Aitor flagged supplier responsiveness as a potential structural limitation on 24 June and no use case has finished end-to-end, so the do-nothing / Workday+Omnia path wins by default until ANA delivers measurable results.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 46.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Live weekly cadence: ANA PoC follow-ups are booked 9, 16 and 23 July and Aitor endorsed the supplier-response fix and asked for a delivery date on 1 July - a buyer pull, not a push.
CloseDate slipped twice in a month (4 Jun to 30 Jun, then 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 29 June) and the executive go/no-go is still 'TBD', so momentum is real but the date is not.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Services/intangible-spend accuracy - McAfee's most-spent category - is the one criterion where Zycus conceded it is 'not that accurate' (Devika, 21 October 2025), so the POC must close that exposure to win.
Reference risk: the Nippon Gas call landed but was not close enough to McAfee's use case, and Tata Play (the buyer's #1) is still pending (Aitor, 24 June 2026).

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Recency-weighted, the single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing / status-quo path (Workday sourcing + Omnia orchestration): with no POC use case completed end-to-end and supplier responsiveness flagged as a potential structural…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: McAfee narrowed 9 vendors to 3 for the AI/ANA evaluation, signed the POC agreement (April 2026), and is relaunching use cases with test suppliers - enterprise-grade commitment to the evaluation.

Commitment stops short of budget: deployment is explicitly conditional on POC success ('it depends on how well it goes to POC', Aitor 21 October 2025) and the written commercial proposal is still undelivered.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Send Aitor a committed delivery date for the email/portal supplier-response enhancement. (by 10 Jul)
Complete the first ANA use case end-to-end and capture McAfee's own savings and throughput numbers. (by 17 Jul)
Facilitate the Tata Play reference call, the buyer's number-one requested reference. (by 17 Jul)

calls_omitted.reason: No transcript/notes available in the record
No transcript/notes available in the record

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d; field history trailing; contact roles; Next_Step/Next_Step_History
gaps: Economic buyer / CFO named and engaged - referenced in Next Steps but never mapped to a Salesforce contact (knowledge gap).
Peer POC vendors in the final 3 remain unnamed (Aitor, 17 October 2025) - blocks tailored competitive positioning.
No McAfee-specific POC savings/throughput baseline captured yet (no use case completed end-to-end).
